```cpp
class BookMyShow {
private:
    struct SegmentTreeNode {
        int l, r;
        long long total;
        int firstEmpty;
        SegmentTreeNode* left;
        SegmentTreeNode* right;

        SegmentTreeNode(int l, int r) : l(l), r(r), total(0), firstEmpty(0), left(nullptr), right(nullptr) {}
    };

    SegmentTreeNode* root;
    int m;

    void build(SegmentTreeNode* node) {
        if (node->l == node->r) return;
        int mid = (node->l + node->r) / 2;
        node->left = new SegmentTreeNode(node->l, mid);
        node->right = new SegmentTreeNode(mid + 1, node->r);
        build(node->left);
        build(node->right);
    }

    void pushDown(SegmentTreeNode* node) {
        if (node->left == nullptr || node->right == nullptr) return;
        if (node->total == 0) {
            node->left->firstEmpty = node->left->l;
            node->right->firstEmpty = node->right->l;
        } else {
            node->left->firstEmpty = node->left->l;
            node->right->firstEmpty = node->right->l;
        }
    }

    void update(SegmentTreeNode* node, int idx, int val) {
        if (node->l == node->r) {
            node->total += val;
            node->firstEmpty = (node->total == 0) ? node->l : node->firstEmpty;
            return;
        }
        pushDown(node);
        if (idx <= node->left->r) update(node->left, idx, val);
        else update(node->right, idx, val);
        node->total = node->left->total + node->right->total;
        node->firstEmpty = (node->left->total == 0) ? node->left->firstEmpty : node->right->firstEmpty;
    }

    vector<int> gatherQuery(SegmentTreeNode* node, int k, int maxRow) {
        if (node->l > maxRow || node->total + k > (long long)(node->r - node->l + 1) * m) return {};
        if (node->l == node->r) {
            if (m - node->total >= k) return {node->l, node->total};
            return {};
        }
        pushDown(node);
        vector<int> leftRes = gatherQuery(node->left, k, maxRow);
        if (!leftRes.empty()) return leftRes;
        return gatherQuery(node->right, k, maxRow);
    }

    bool scatterQuery(SegmentTreeNode* node, int k, int maxRow) {
        if (node->l > maxRow || node->total >= k) return false;
        if (node->l == node->r) {
            if (m - node->total >= k) {
                update(node, node->l, k);
                return true;
            }
            return false;
        }
        pushDown(node);
        if (node->left->total >= k) {
            if (scatterQuery(node->left, k, maxRow)) return true;
        } else {
            int leftTake = node->left->total;
            if (scatterQuery(node->left, leftTake, maxRow)) {
                if (scatterQuery(node->right, k - leftTake, maxRow)) return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

public:
    BookMyShow(int n, int m) {
        this->m = m;
        root = new SegmentTreeNode(0, n - 1);
        build(root);
    }

    vector<int> gather(int k, int maxRow) {
        vector<int> res = gatherQuery(root, k, maxRow);
        if (!res.empty()) {
            update(root, res[0], k);
        }
        return res;
    }

    bool scatter(int k, int maxRow) {
        return scatterQuery(root, k, maxRow);
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n) for gather and scatter operations
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a segment tree to efficiently manage seat allocations for both gather and scatter operations. The segment tree tracks the number of available seats in each row and allows for efficient querying and updating. For gather, it finds the smallest row with enough consecutive seats. For scatter, it allocates seats across rows in the smallest row numbers, ensuring the total number of seats meets the group size. The segment tree ensures logarithmic time complexity for both operations.
